qudipy.circuit package¶

Submodules¶

qudipy.circuit.control_pulse module¶

Class for a control pulse

@author: simba

class qudipy.circuit.control_pulse.ControlPulse(pulse_name, pulse_type, pulse_length=-1, ideal_gate=None, gates=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

add_control_variable(var_name, var_pulse)[source]¶

Adds a control variable to the pulse. If the variable name is time, then it will store the time points in the ctrl_time variable.

var_namestring

Name of new control variable being added.

var_pulse1D array

DESCRIPTION.

None.

add_mult_gates()[source]¶

Changes gates attribute to be a QuantumCircuit of the correct format for the gates specified in the parameter add_gates.

add_gates: List/Tuple/Arrayof(String)

A list of pulse names that we wish to have run simultaneously

None

This function does not create the pulses necessary for these gates, it just flags that the ControlPulse contains a pulse with more than one type of gate acting on multiple qubits simultaneously.

copy()[source]¶

This method will do a deep copy of the current class object.

ControlPulse

Deep copy of current ControlPulse object.

plot(plot_ctrls='all', time_int='full', n=250)[source]¶

Plot the control pulse. Can plot a subset of the control variables and within some time interval.

plot_ctrlslist of strings, optional

Specify the name of each control variable pulse you wish to plot or plot ‘all’. The default is ‘all’.

time_intlist of floats

Specify the time interval over which to plot the pulse. Cannot be less than 0 or greater than the current pulse length. The default is the full time interval.

nint, optional

Number of points to use in the pulse when plotting. The default is 250.

None.

print_pulse()[source]¶

Prints a circuit diagram of the pulse

  • Prints to screen

None

set_pulse_length(pulse_length)[source]¶

Change the pulse length by scaling the time axis of the pulse with respect to the previously specified pulse.

pulse_lengthint

Pulse legnth for control pulse in [s].

None.

qudipy.circuit.file_parsers module¶

Functions for handling control pulse files .ctrlp

@author: simba

qudipy.circuit.file_parsers.check_ideal_gate(gate_name, qubit_idx=None)[source]¶

This function checks if the supplied gate_name is a valid ideal gate keyword used to simulate an ideal quantum circuit. Current supported keywords are I, RX###, RY###, RZ###, H, CTRLX, CTRLY, CTRLZ, SWAP, RSWAP, R(x,y,z)###, and negative rotations are supported by RX-###, RY-###, RZ-###, R(x,y,z)-### where ### in the R gates indicates the gate’s rotation angle in degrees.

gate_namestring

Gate name to be tested.

qubit_idxlist of ints, optional

Indices of qubits used by gate. Default is [].

boolean

qudipy.circuit.file_parsers.load_circuit(f_name, gate_dict=None)[source]¶

This function takes in a single quantum circuit .qcirc file as input and constructs a quantum circuit object

f_namestring

Full file path to the circuit file.

gate_dictdictionary of controlPulse objects, optional

Contains all loaded control pulse objects to be used in the quantumCircuit’s circuit sequence. Default is {}.

q_circQuantumCircuit object

Class containing information loaded from the .qcirc file required for simulation.

qudipy.circuit.file_parsers.load_pulses(f_names)[source]¶

This function takes multiple .ctrlp files as inputs and constructs a dictionary containing every control pulse’s information

f_nameslist of strings

Full file path to the control pulse file to load.

pulse_dictdictionary of ControlPulse objects

Dictionary containing many control pulse objects

qudipy.circuit.quantum_circuit module¶

Class for a quantum circuit

@author: simba

class qudipy.circuit.quantum_circuit.QuantumCircuit(circuit_name, n_qubits, pulse_dict)[source]¶

Bases: object

add_gate(gate_name, ideal_gate, used_qubits, position=None, mult_gates=False)[source]¶

This function adds a gate into the quantum circuit sequence.

gate_nameAnyof(string, tuple(string))

Name of gate being added to sequence. If it is a tuple, then it contains the strings of the names of the multiple gates added simultaneously.

ideal_gate: Anyof(string, tupleof(string), None)

“IDEAL_GATE” if the gate is ideal, and None if it is not an ideal gate.

used_qubitsAnyof(int list, tuple(int list))

Indices of qubits acted on by the gate(s) where a tuple of lists indicates multiple gates acting simultaneously.

position: Int, None, optional

The positive integer greater than 0 that specifies where the gate should be added to the circuit. If position is None, the gate will added onto the end.

mult_gates: Bool, optional

The predicate that affirms if there are multiple gates acting in the circut simultaneously. Default is False.

None.

get_next_gate()[source]¶

Get the next gate in the quantum circuit sequence. If no more exist, return None.

next_gate[string, int list]

Returns a list containing the next gate name and the affected qubits by the gate.

load_more_gates(pulse_dict)[source]¶

Adds more controlPulse objects to the gate dictionary.

pulse_dictdict of controlPulse objects

Dictionary containing all the pulse objects to be added.

None.

print_ideal_circuit()[source]¶

Prints out an ascii display of the loaded circuit sequence for the user.

None.

None.

reset_circuit_index()[source]¶

Reset the current gate index for the circuit sequence back to the begining of the circuit.

None.

qudipy.circuit.write_files module¶

Module to write .ctrlp files and .qcirc files from the respective ContrsolPulse and QuantumCircuit Objects

@author: Madi

qudipy.circuit.write_files.format_single_gate(gate)[source]¶

Formats a gate into a writable string

gate: Listof(Str, Listof(Int))

The length 3 list that contains one gate inside of a QuantumCircuit sequence

gate is in the following form: [‘gate_name’, ‘Ideal Gate’, [effected qubits]]

String

used to write the gate in the .qcirc file

qudipy.circuit.write_files.write_ctrlp(CntrlPulse, print_ctrlp=True)[source]¶

Writes a .ctrlp file from the ControlPulse object passed

CntrlPulse: ControlPulse Object

Specifies what information to add into the .ctrlp file

print_qcirc: Bool

If True, the function will print out the .ctrlp file. If False, nothing will be printed

Returns a .ctrlp file containing the information in the ControlPulse object

Prints to screen:

Prints the .ctrlp file to screen

  • A valid ControlPulse class object as a parameter defined in the directory qudipy/circuit/control_pulse.py

qudipy.circuit.write_files.write_qcirc(QntmCirc, print_qcirc=True)[source]¶

Writes a .qcirc object representing the Quantum Circuit object passed

QntmCirc: QuantumCircuit

The QuantumCircuit object that is to be written into a .qcirc file

print_qcirc: Bool, optional

The predicate determining if the user wants the .qcirc file to be printed to screen. Default is True

Returns a .qcirc file

  • A valid QuantumCircuit class object is passed as per the class defined with the directory qudipy/circuit/quantum_circuit.py

Module contents¶

Module for handling pulse generation
